title: Clerodendrum inerme
intro: Clerodendrum inerme, also known as the 'Sea Borne Glory', is a tropical flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin ailments, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Clerodendrum inerme include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. In traditional medicine, Clerodendrum inerme is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Clerodendrum inerme is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae and is native to tropical Asia, specifically in the coastal regions of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It is also known as the 'Sea Mos Fernandez' or 'Tropical Bleeding Heart'. This plant belongs to the genus Clerodendrum, which includes around 450 species of flowering plants. The family Lamiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the mint family. Clerodendrum inerme is a vigorous and fast-growing shrub or small tree that can grow up to 6 meters in height.

side_effects: Clerodendrum inerme, also known as the sea Moses or coastal clerodendrum, is a plant native to tropical regions.
